scenario.notes: Nationally recognized Northeast multi-species lake with major bass and pike relevance.

report.summary_line: Some things are helping, and some are getting in the way. Wind gives you a real opening, although temperature still keeps the day tight.
report.actionable_tip: This is a good day to stay just a touch more patient than normal: clean pace, softer moves, and no wasted action.
report.daypart_note: Clear skies mean sharp light transitions. Dawn and evening are your prime windows.

scenario.notes: Nationally known tailwater trout fishery with strong mainstream pressure.

report.summary_line: This is a day for patience and good decisions. Cloud cover is the clearest positive, while temperature is the clearest problem. On a river day, staying disciplined usually matters more than covering too much water.
report.actionable_tip: Do not force the presentation. A smooth, slightly slower retrieve is the better starting point when temperature is only close.
report.daypart_note: Fish the edges — dawn and evening are your best bets when other signals are flat.

scenario.notes: Extremely popular Texas flats benchmark for redfish and trout.

report.summary_line: The setup is mostly helpful today. Tidal movement is the main thing pushing the setup in the right direction. This is a trustworthy read, but it is better as direction than over-precision.
report.actionable_tip: Use the current as part of the retrieve: cast a little uptide, let the bait swing, and add only small corrections.
report.daypart_note: The middle of the day is where conditions line up best today.
